channel.title: The Grant Desk - Visual & Media Arts, Worldwide, next 90 days
channel.description: Paid open calls, fellowships and residencies in AI, tech, research, and digital and mixed-media arts, sorted into the right pile. Updated as new calls land on the desk. Filtered to: Visual & Media Arts, Worldwide, next 90 days.

item.title: Eamonn McCabe Bursary 2026 (Royal Photographic Society)
item.description:
Organisation: The Royal Photographic Society (RPS), in partnership with Eamonn McCabe's family and The Guardian

Location: International bursary. Applicants must be aged 30 years or under on the closing date. Interviews held via Zoom.

Award: GBP 3,000 to deliver a photography project on the theme of 'sporting endeavour'. The completed project will be published by The Guardian and the RPS, who will work with the recipient to bring the project to a wider audience.

Duration: The project should be completed within one year of being awarded.

Deadline: 03 Aug 2026

The Eamonn McCabe Bursary, launched in 2024, commemorates the life of photojournalist Eamonn McCabe (1948-2022), who made his name as a sports photographer before moving to wider editorial work, mainly for The Observer and The Guardian, and who supported a generation of photographers from the 1980s to the 2020s. Run by the RPS in partnership with McCabe's family and The Guardian, this international bursary continues that legacy. AWARD: GBP 3,000 to a photographer aged 30 years or under to deliver a photography project responding to the theme of 'sporting endeavour', which can be interpreted as widely as the applicant wishes, though the general approach should be suitable for publication. The project should normally be new, not previously exhibited or published. As well as professional sports, the call particularly encourages projects covering grassroots and community activities (for example, addressing issues such as mental health or supporting displaced communities), and welcomes creative interpretations of the theme. The completed project will be published by The Guardian and the RPS. ELIGIBILITY: applicants must be aged 30 years or under on the closing date. PROCESS: interviews via Zoom in September; recipient announced by the end of September; project completed within one year of award. Apply via the RPS application portal using RPS Account credentials. Closing date 3 August 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Textile Exchange x Atmos: Fashion Regenerated 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Textile Exchange (with Atmos)

Location: Open call; work to be promoted globally through Textile Exchange and Atmos and exhibited at the 2026 Textile Exchange conference.

Award: Overall winner: a paid collaborative commission worth USD 10,000 with Textile Exchange to develop a new body of work documenting the evidence of regeneration, published through Atmos and Textile Exchange and shown at the 2026 conference. Two runners-up: work promoted by Textile Exchange and displayed at the conference exhibition, plus a USD 2,000 cash prize.

Duration: One-off commission/prize.

Deadline: 20 Jul 2026

Fashion Regenerated is an open call from Textile Exchange and Atmos. Winners are selected by a panel of expert judges and, for the first time, a public vote. The shortlist is promoted through Textile Exchange and Atmos, amplifying inspirational stories to an engaged audience of thousands. The overall winner receives a paid collaborative commission to the value of USD 10,000 with Textile Exchange to develop a new body of work documenting the evidence of regeneration, working closely with the team to identify the right story and access; the winning work is published through Atmos, Textile Exchange, and shown to an audience of more than 1,300 changemakers at the 2026 Textile Exchange conference. Two runners-up have their work promoted by Textile Exchange and displayed at the conference exhibition, and there is also a USD 2,000 cash prize. Check the call site for full eligibility and submission details. Deadline: 20 July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Foundwork Artist Prize 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Foundwork

Location: Open call. No location restriction stated; submitted online via Foundwork.

Award: The honoree receives an unrestricted USD 10,000 grant, studio visits with each of the five jurors, and an interview for Foundwork's Dialogues program. Three artists named to the Short List each receive a USD 1,000 micro-grant.

Duration: One-off annual juried award.

Deadline: 25 Sep 2026

The Foundwork Artist Prize is an annual juried award supporting outstanding artistic practices. The 2026 honoree receives an unrestricted USD 10,000 grant, studio visits with each of the five jurors, and an interview for Foundwork's Dialogues program. In addition, three artists are named to the 2026 Short List, each receiving a USD 1,000 micro-grant in support of their practice. Submissions are made online through Foundwork. Application deadline 25 September 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: OIF: Support for Artist Mobility and Circulation of Cultural Goods 2026 (Francophonie)
item.description:
Organisation: Organisation internationale de la Francophonie (OIF)

Location: Francophone zone (OIF member states globally)

Award: Up to EUR 5,000 for artist mobility (travel, accommodation, visas, logistics). Up to EUR 2,500 for circulation of cultural goods (excess baggage, freight, customs). Both can be combined. No application fee.

Duration: Residencies or tours of 5 days to 3 months. Projects must take place between 25 March and 30 November 2026.

Deadline: 31 Aug 2026

The OIF Support for Artist Mobility and Circulation of Cultural Goods programme provides grants of up to EUR 5,000 for mobility (travel, accommodation, visa fees, logistics) and up to EUR 2,500 for circulation of cultural goods (excess baggage, freight, customs) within the Francophone zone; both can be combined. ELIGIBILITY: professional artists and established artistic collectives with a demonstrated connection to the Francophone space (by nationality, working language, or artistic collaborations), residing in an OIF member state, with minimum 2 years of experience in the cultural or artistic sector; must directly manage the project (not act as intermediaries); one application per individual per call. ELIGIBLE DISCIPLINES: live performance (music, theatre, dance, comedy, storytelling), cinema/audiovisual, literature/publishing, and visual arts. PRIORITY given to projects promoting gender equality, women artists, creators under 35, and South-South mobility. Projects must take place between 25 March and 30 November 2026. Multiple selection sessions throughout the year; call open until 31 August 2026. Information webinars: 11 March, 13 May, and 8 July 2026 at 3 PM Paris time. Contact: culture.dlc@francophonie.org. Apply at https://appelsaprojets.francophonie.org/fr/applications/soutien-a-la-circulation-des-biens-et-mobilite-des-artistes.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Lee Ufan Arles: Prix Art & Environnement 2026 (Residency + Exhibition Prize)
item.description:
Organisation: Lee Ufan Arles and Maison Guerlain

Location: Arles, France (residency and exhibition)

Award: Funded residency (production space + accommodation) in Arles. No cash prize amount specified. No application fee.

Duration: 6–8 weeks residency in early 2027, followed by a solo summer 2027 exhibition

Deadline: 31 Jul 2026

The Prix Art & Environnement (4th edition), awarded by Lee Ufan Arles and Maison Guerlain, supports an artist working at the intersection of art and the natural environment. The prize winner receives a funded 6–8 week production residency in Arles in early 2027, including production space, accommodation, and mentorship from Lee Ufan Arles staff, culminating in a solo summer 2027 exhibition. ELIGIBILITY: open to all artists of any discipline and nationality, with no age or degree requirements; candidates must demonstrate at least one solo exhibition and one or more group exhibitions. DEADLINE: 31 July 2026. APPLY via the Google Form linked from the Lee Ufan Arles website.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: eidolon Grant 2026 (Vernacular Photography)
item.description:
Organisation: eidolon

Location: International; open worldwide.

Award: A total of EUR 25,000 to be distributed across two application categories. No application fee.

Duration: Annual grant; supported projects contribute to the institution's program in the coming year

Deadline: 30 Sep 2026

The eidolon Grant is an international programme, presented annually, for projects that explore, promote and conserve vernacular photography. It is open to artists, academics, professionals, researchers, collectors and vernacular photography enthusiasts whose past work and proposed project is centred around the image heritage of everyday photography. The grant aims to identify phenomena, collections, histories, practices and trends within vernacular photography, offering new interpretations and analyses, and thematising both photographic heritages and contemporary photographic practices. Applications can be made under two categories, with a total of EUR 25,000 to be distributed. Each chosen project will contribute to the enrichment of eidolon's program in the coming year. The deadline for submission is 30 September 2026 at 11:59 p.m. Apply via https://everydayphotography.org/centre/grant-2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: PhotoVogue Global Open Call 2026: Brave New Visions - Creativity as Rebellion
item.description:
Organisation: PhotoVogue (Conde Nast)

Location: Online (global)

Award: $12,000 in total grants split across three artists: a $6,000 Outstanding Vision Grant, a $4,000 Vision Grant, and a $2,000 Rising Voice Grant (emerging), plus presentation at the next PhotoVogue Festival, potential publication across Vogue's global editions, and PhotoVogue Virtual Portfolio Reviews. Free to submit.

Duration: One-off open call

Deadline: 11 Sep 2026

PhotoVogue's global open call invites photographers and video makers to use image-making as a form of rebellion - to challenge indifference, disrupt conventions and expand visual storytelling. It asks not for a theme but for a position in relation to the world. Open to photography, video and multimedia projects across all genres (fashion, documentary, portraiture, fine art, experimental). Three artists share $12,000 in grants ($6,000 Outstanding Vision, $4,000 Vision, $2,000 Rising Voice for an emerging artist), with festival presentation, potential Vogue publication and portfolio reviews. ELIGIBILITY: open to all artists worldwide aged 18+; submissions are free; previous PhotoVogue applicants may submit a new project. SUBMIT: a series of up to 15 images, or images plus video for multimedia, and/or a 60-second trailer, via Picter. IMPORTANT: AI-generated works are NOT eligible. Open 14 May to 11 September 2026 (11:59 PM CET).

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Beautiful Bizarre Art Prize 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Beautiful Bizarre Magazine

Location: Worldwide (exhibition in San Francisco, USA)

Award: Grand Prize $10,000 plus category awards (e.g. Imaginative Realism $5,000) and a dedicated Digital Art Award and Emerging Artist Award; $76,000+ total in cash and prizes. NOTE: $40 entry fee per submission.

Duration: Annual prize (exhibition follows)

Deadline: 17 Jul 2026

International, non-acquisitive art prize across seven categories including a dedicated Digital Art Award, with cash prizes and a San Francisco gallery exhibition. Grand Prize is $10,000, with category awards and over $76,000 in total cash and prizes. ELIGIBILITY: open internationally with no geographic restriction; all static media; includes an Emerging Artist Award. NOTE: there is a $40 entry fee per submission.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).
